description: Personal Budget Tracker — SQLite-backed, single-user app to manage transactions and categories, set monthly spending limits, detect over-budget categories, and visualise spending on a dashboard.

userStories.title: Database schema and migrations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the SQLite schema created on startup so the app can persist data immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: db.ts opens (or creates) budget.db using bun:sqlite (import { Database } from 'bun:sqlite')
categories table: id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, monthly_limit REAL (nullable)
transactions table: id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, amount REAL NOT NULL, date TEXT NOT NULL (ISO-8601), description TEXT, category_id INTEGER REFERENCES categories(id)
Both tables created with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS
Running bun index.ts twice does not error or drop data
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Category API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need REST endpoints for categories so the frontend can manage them.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/categories returns JSON array of all categories (id, name, monthly_limit)
POST /api/categories with { name, monthly_limit? } creates a category; returns 201 with the new row
PUT /api/categories/:id with { name?, monthly_limit? } updates a category; returns 200 with updated row
DELETE /api/categories/:id deletes the category; returns 204; returns 409 if transactions reference it
All handlers live in api/categories.ts and are imported in index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Transaction API endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I need REST endpoints for transactions so the frontend can list, add, and delete them.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/transactions returns JSON array ordered by date DESC; each item includes id, amount, date, description, category_id, category_name
GET /api/transactions?month=YYYY-MM filters results to that month
POST /api/transactions with { amount, date, description?, category_id } creates a transaction; returns 201
DELETE /api/transactions/:id deletes a transaction; returns 204
All handlers live in api/transactions.ts and are imported in index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: App shell and tab navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want a persistent navigation bar so I can move between Dashboard, Transactions, and Categories.
acceptanceCriteria: index.html loads frontend/App.tsx via <script type="module">
Tailwind CSS applied via CDN in index.html
App renders a top nav with three tabs: Dashboard, Transactions, Categories
Active tab is visually highlighted with Tailwind classes
Switching tabs renders the correct page component without a full page reload (client-side state)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Categories page — list, add, edit, delete
userStories.description: As a user, I want to manage my spending categories so I can organise transactions and set monthly limits.
acceptanceCriteria: Categories page lists all categories with name and monthly_limit (shows '—' if none)
Inline add form with name (required text) and monthly limit (optional number) fields; submits via POST /api/categories
Edit button opens an inline edit form pre-filled with current values; Save submits via PUT /api/categories/:id
Delete button submits via DELETE /api/categories/:id; if response is 409, shows error message 'Cannot delete: category has transactions'
Category list re-fetches and re-renders after each create, update, or delete without full page reload
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Transactions page — list, add, delete
userStories.description: As a user, I want to log and remove transactions so I can track my spending.
acceptanceCriteria: Transactions page lists all transactions: date, description, amount (formatted as $X,XXX.XX), category name; ordered newest first
Inline add form: amount (required number), date (required date input defaulting to today), description (optional text), category (required dropdown populated from GET /api/categories)
Submit adds transaction via POST /api/transactions; new row appears at top of list without page reload
Delete button on each row removes transaction via DELETE /api/transactions/:id; row disappears immediately
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Over-budget alert component
userStories.description: As a user, I want a prominent alert when I've exceeded a category's monthly limit so I can adjust my spending.
acceptanceCriteria: BudgetAlerts.tsx component accepts a list of { categoryName, limit, actual } objects as props
For each over-budget entry (actual > limit), renders a visible red alert: '⚠ [Category]: $X.XX spent of $Y.YY limit'
Component renders nothing (no DOM output) when the list is empty or no entries are over-budget
BudgetAlerts is rendered at the top of the Dashboard page, above the charts
Over-budget determination: sum of transaction amounts for a category where date starts with current YYYY-MM > monthly_limit
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dashboard — spending by category chart
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see how much I've spent by category this month so I can identify where my money is going.
acceptanceCriteria: Install recharts via bun install recharts
SpendingChart.tsx renders a bar chart or pie chart of total spending per category for the current calendar month
Chart is labelled with category names and dollar amounts
Categories with zero spending for the month are excluded from the chart
Chart title reads 'Spending by Category — [Month YYYY]'
Chart data fetched from GET /api/transactions?month=YYYY-MM
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dashboard — month-over-month totals chart
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my total monthly spending over the last 12 months so I can spot trends.
acceptanceCriteria: MonthOverMonthChart.tsx renders a bar chart of total spending per month for the last 12 calendar months (including current month)
X-axis labelled with month abbreviations (e.g. 'Mar', 'Apr'); Y-axis shows dollar amounts
Months with no transactions display a zero bar (no gaps in the chart)
Chart title reads 'Monthly Spending — Last 12 Months'
Data fetched from GET /api/transactions (no month filter); monthly aggregation done client-side
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Dashboard — category breakdown table
userStories.description: As a user, I want a table comparing each category's limit to actual spending so I can see my budget status at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: CategoryBreakdownTable.tsx renders a table with columns: Category | Monthly Limit | Spent This Month | Remaining
Monthly Limit column shows 'No limit' when monthly_limit is null
Remaining = limit − spent; shows '—' when there is no limit; shown in red text when negative
Rows sorted alphabetically by category name (A–Z)
Table rendered in the Dashboard page below the two charts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
